question.title: Average Order Value by Region
question.desc: Calculate the average order value for each region by joining the orders table with the customers table and grouping the results by region.
question.setup:
DROP TABLE IF EXISTS customers;
      DROP TABLE IF EXISTS orders;
      CREATE TABLE customers (
         customer_id SERIAL PRIMARY KEY,
         name VARCHAR(255),
         region VARCHAR(255)
      );
      CREATE TABLE orders (
         order_id SERIAL PRIMARY KEY,
         customer_id INTEGER,
         order_value DECIMAL(10, 2),
         FOREIGN KEY (customer_id) REFERENCES customers(customer_id)
      );
      INSERT INTO customers (customer_id, name, region)
      VALUES
         (1, 'John Doe', 'North'),
         (2, 'Jane Doe', 'South'),
         (3, 'Bob Smith', 'North'),
         (4, 'Alice Johnson', 'East'),
         (5, 'Mike Brown', 'West');
      INSERT INTO orders (order_id, customer_id, order_value)
      VALUES
         (1, 1, 100.00),
         (2, 1, 200.00),
         (3, 2, 50.00),
         (4, 3, 150.00),
         (5, 4, 300.00),
         (6, 5, 250.00),
         (7, 1, 400.00),
         (8, 2, 75.00),
         (9, 3, 125.00),
         (10, 4, 225.00);
question.solution:
SELECT 
         c.region, 
         AVG(o.order_value) AS average_order_value
      FROM 
         customers c
      JOIN 
         orders o ON c.customer_id = o.customer_id
      GROUP BY 
         c.region;
question.tips: Use the AVG function to calculate the average order value and join the tables based on the customer_id.
hints: You need to join two tables to get the required information.
Use the GROUP BY clause to group the results by region.
The AVG function will be used to calculate the average order value for each region.
